sources_exist.note: All 7 canonical URL occurrences resolve to accessible pages. However, each citation is unsupported in whole or in the scope required by its input.

figures_match_sources.note: Autophoto reports 14% of ecommerce shops using AI image manipulation and 60-70% cost reductions for certain imagery, but not the quoted 25% respondent statistic or Zenfolio weekly-use figure. Zenfolio supports almost 50% weekly AI use mainly for admin, but its accessible page does not expose the claimed 28% age-60+ statistic. Wikipedia does not establish a commercial-photographer majority-adoption date. John Salony supports 1.5-3.0x SDE and PE buyer ranges of 3.5-5.0x SDE, not the cited 4-9x EBITDA or the run's normalized multiples. Wonderful Machine's cited page does not contain the quoted assignment.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Role fractions are bounded and generally plausible, but current_adoption_pct=0.25 conflates ecommerce shops, marketers, and all photographers with US 541922 firms materially using production AI. The t50 derivation is not reproducible: a 10-year analog times 0.4 yields a four-year total from a 2024 baseline, which would imply about two years remaining in 2026, not 3.5. pi_dist and pi_moat also depend on unsupported 50/50 billing and physical-truth segment shares.

rubric_consistent.note: inputs.active_consolidators.value is 1 although its quoted finding says no PE-backed platform was identified as actively rolling up commercial photography in the last 24 months. Multiples do not satisfy the frozen EBITDA-normalization rule. These defects are decisive for a record only 0.0240 below the pass cut; the assigned within-tolerance arithmetic deltas do not resolve them.

cross_checks_honest.note: The no-proxy and terminal-demand distinctions are responsive, but the pricing cross-check states an unsupported 50/50 revenue split, unsupported $3-12 versus $85-250 unit costs, and an uncited Getty decline as confirmation. The terminal class relies on an unsupported 40-55% physical-truth revenue share.

source_audits.note: The broker page describes generic buyer types for photography studios but names no active consolidator or qualifying add-on in the last 24 months. It does not mention CADY, Jostens, Herff Jones, ImageQuix, or Aryeo. The record's own quote says no PE-backed commercial-photography platform was found, contradicting value=1.

The page reports 1.5-3.0x SDE for photography studios and explains that EBITDA is typically used for larger $5M+ revenue absentee-owned firms. It does not report a 4-9x EBITDA range, a $80-100K owner replacement cost, a $100K normalized-EBITDA example, or a 4.0x EV/EBITDA transaction median.

The vendor aggregation states that 14% of ecommerce shops use AI for image manipulation. It does not contain the quoted 25% of respondents using generative AI for photography work or the nearly-50% photographer weekly-use statistic, and ecommerce-shop adoption is not the required share of US commercial-photography firms materially adopting AI.
The page reports photography-studio SDE ranges, including 3.5-5.0x+ for PE buyers, but no 4-9x EBITDA range, 6-9x creative-services platform comp, $10M+ EBITDA photography exit, or evidence for 7.0x.

The article documents digital-photography history and professional workflow advantages, but it does not establish that commercial photographers moved from about 1998 to majority adoption in about 2008 or report a 2006-2010 majority-workflow threshold.

The accessible 2026 industry-report page covers all photography types and reports almost 50% weekly AI use, mainly for administrative tasks. Its accessible text does not state that respondents aged 60+ are 28%, and no evidence is supplied that an all-photographer response demographic measures owners of US 541922 employer firms.

This is a consulting-service and case-archive landing page. It does not contain the quoted six-day athleisure assignment, $2,500/day fee, $19,800 usage license, or the run's 50/50 revenue split. The AI unit-cost figures come from a different source and are not established by this cited URL.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://autophoto.ai/blog/ai-product-photography-photo-editing-stats/), replace the mixed ecommerce/marketer/photographer statistics with evidence for material production-AI adoption among US 541922 firms, then recompute the derivation of inputs.t50_years so its timeline is mathematically reproducible.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_photography), provide a source that actually measures the film-to-digital time to majority adoption for comparable commercial-photography workflows rather than inferring an unsupported 1998-2008 threshold.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://zenfolio.com/sopi/2026-april/), provide an accessible table or report excerpt for the 28% figure and establish whether respondents are owners and whether the commercial-photography population is comparable.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.johnsalony.com/photography), reconcile value=1 with the quoted conclusion that no active PE-backed commercial-photography platform was found, and provide named qualifying add-on evidence within the last 24 months for every platform counted.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.johnsalony.com/photography), do not convert generic 1.5-3.0x and 3.5-5.0x SDE ranges into unsupported 4.0x and 7.0x EV/EBITDA inputs; provide size-comparable transaction evidence after market-rate owner compensation for each tier.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://wonderfulmachine.com/consulting/pricing-and-negotiating/), cite the exact accessible case supporting the assignment economics and provide evidence for the industry-wide 50% day-rate/50% licensing mix; then reassess inputs.pi_dist and the pricing cross-check.

At cross_checks.terminal_value and inputs.pi_moat, substantiate the asserted 40-55% physical-truth share of 541922 revenue with segment evidence; the current sources establish substitution pressure but not the revenue weighting that determines PRESSURED versus MELTING.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, S, V
